impact: BASS=0.65 (the primary calibration capture) is clean at RMS 1.02dB, but BASS=0.50/0.35 show ~3dB error at 250-430Hz. Ruled out: C27 100n->82n had zero effect (the old audit recommended exactly this; it had already been tested). The hump correlates with the MID shift throw (430Hz), not BASS Q -- C27/C29 tests confirm the error is pre-tone-stack. Look at the MID stage and wet path, not the BASS rail.
title: V2 BASS 250-430Hz hump at low BASS settings
